Nice big apples. You might have a hard time picking them, but once they've fallen they're fair game. If you live nearby and can walk here, no-one will notice. If you're driving in, there's guest parking spots south of the field, which you can't park in overnig

There are dispersed vines here that (I'm pretty sure) are grapes, but be extra careful. Toxic lookalikes like canada moonseed and virginia creeper exist in this area as well. Be careful for pesticides/pollution, and wash carefully before consumption Access: Pu

Good fruit hedges around car-park, quickly eaten by the birds during its season though. Careful for pesticides/pollution.

There are several grape vines that give fruit around here during the fall. When I tried them in early September, they were still somewhat sour. They were sparse also. Check for pesticides.

Large amount of frost grapes near the bridge, very tasty. Watch out for possible pesticide contamination. Access: Public.
